task.title: Persist task cancellation reason
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/443 by persisting one opaque non-empty reason on new cancelled tasks while preserving replay compatibility for legacy empty cancellation events and leaving structured cancellation and runtime policy undefined.
acceptance_criteria: Cancelling an active task requires a non-empty UTF-8 reason, preserves its exact content, and reloads it after reopening SQLite.
New task.cancelled events use payload version two while legacy empty payload-version-one cancellations remain replayable without a reason.
Empty caller and persisted reasons are rejected explicitly, and non-cancelled task states expose no cancellation reason.
Existing missing-task, terminal-conflict, race, completion-output, failure-diagnostic, invalid-history, and explicit replay-error behavior remains intact.
Public behavior and non-goals are documented, pinned verification passes, and required pull-request gates permit squash auto-merge.

attempts.summary: Created bounded issue 443 and wrote cancellation-reason lifecycle assertions before adding production API support.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused RED command failed with unresolved TaskCancellation, an unexpected second argument to the old TaskStore::cancel API, and a missing Task::cancellation accessor.
attempts.patch: Added reopening and direct-payload tests that required a validated reason and asserted the returned, persisted, and replayed task retained it.

attempts.summary: Evolved cancellation with an explicitly versioned reason payload.
attempts.diagnostic: New writes stored payload version two with exact whitespace-preserving reason JSON. A dedicated compatibility test replayed an empty version-one cancellation as cancelled state with no reason, while an empty version-two reason surfaced an explicit malformed-payload replay error.
attempts.patch: Added TaskCancellation and TaskCancellationError, optional cancellation data on Task, a required reason argument on TaskStore::cancel, and version-aware task.cancelled encoding and decoding.

attempts.summary: Retained existing terminal lifecycle behavior and documented the bounded cancellation contract.
attempts.diagnostic: All 31 task lifecycle integration tests passed, including completion-cancellation races, terminal conflicts, legacy replay, malformed history, completion output, failure diagnostics, and reason absence on active, completed, and failed tasks.
attempts.patch: Reused the exact-version terminal append and persisted-winner reload path, updated existing cancellation callers, and documented that reasons remain opaque rather than defining a structured taxonomy, actor, cooperative cancellation, or runtime behavior.

attempts.summary: Ran local and hosted quality gates, monitored automated review, and completed squash auto-merge.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned just verify passed 65 workspace integration tests plus formatting, checking, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff checks. GitHub Rust quality run 29385730444 passed. CodeRabbit passed all pre-merge checks and generated no actionable comments. Pull request 444 squash-merged as a696cba878e77229c51a00c8123368e17dd7955e.
attempts.patch: No correctness correction was required by hosted review; the bounded implementation merged unchanged after local verification.

attempts.summary: Captured and self-validated the merged episode in the Vela-native development corpus.
attempts.diagnostic: The record validator accepted the artifact, complete corpus inspection reported 30 valid and zero invalid records, full pinned verification passed with the record included, and independent read-only review found no blocking record issue.
attempts.patch: Added one sanitized positive record containing the task, compiler-observed RED attempt, diagnostic, compatibility fix, lifecycle rationale, local and hosted verification, review result, lessons, and merged provenance.

outcome.summary: Vela now durably preserves an opaque reason for newly cancelled tasks in a version-two event, replays legacy version-one cancellations without inventing a reason, rejects empty reason data explicitly, and retains serialized terminal transitions without defining structured cancellation or runtime policy.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle persists_cancellation_reason_in_the_typed_event_payload # RED: cancellation reason API was missing
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle # GREEN: 31 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command just verify # 65 workspace integration tests passed before merge
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29385730444 for pull request 444
Required CodeRabbit status for pull request 444 # passed with no actionable comments
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0030-cancellation-reason.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 30 valid, 0 invalid
nix develop --command just verify # development record included
Independent read-only review of issue 445 corpus record # no blocking record issue

lessons: Persisted payload shape changes need a new payload version even when the event discriminator and terminal meaning stay unchanged.
A compatibility decoder can preserve legacy cancelled state honestly by exposing no reason rather than inventing a value or rejecting valid old history.
Opaque validated UTF-8 is a sufficient first cancellation boundary; structured taxonomies, request actors, and cooperative runtime cancellation can remain separate decisions.
Whitespace-preserving tests and direct payload assertions make it explicit that the kernel stores caller reasons without normalization or reinterpretation.
Terminal races should continue using one exact-version append path so adding cancellation data does not weaken persisted-winner conflict reporting.
Symmetric lifecycle data still needs explicit version, compatibility, absence, malformed-payload, and documentation tests rather than relying on analogy alone.
